toolUseResult.originalFile:
################################################################################
# VPC (terraform-aws-modules/vpc)
################################################################################

module "vpc" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"
  version = "~> 5.0"

  name = "${local.name_prefix}-vpc"
  cidr = var.vpc_cidr
  azs  = var.availability_zones

  public_subnets  = [for i, _ in var.availability_zones : cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, i)]
  private_subnets = [for i, _ in var.availability_zones : cidrsubnet(var.vpc_cidr, 8, i + 100)]

  enable_nat_gateway = true
  single_nat_gateway = true

  enable_dns_hostnames = true
  enable_dns_support   = true

  tags = local.common_tags
}

################################################################################
# Security Groups
################################################################################

resource "aws_security_group" "clickhouse" {
  name_prefix = "${local.name_prefix}-clickhouse-"
  description = "Security group for ClickHouse nodes"
  vpc_id      = module.vpc.vpc_id
  tags        = { Name = "${local.name_prefix}-clickhouse-sg" }

  lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true }
}

resource "aws_security_group" "keeper" {
  name_prefix = "${local.name_prefix}-keeper-"
  description = "Security group for ClickHouse Keeper nodes"
  vpc_id      = module.vpc.vpc_id
  tags        = { Name = "${local.name_prefix}-keeper-sg" }

  lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true }
}

resource "aws_security_group" "alb" {
  name_prefix = "${local.name_prefix}-alb-"
  description = "Security group for Application Load Balancer"
  vpc_id      = module.vpc.vpc_id
  tags        = { Name = "${local.name_prefix}-alb-sg" }

  lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true }
}

# --- ClickHouse SG rules ---

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "ch_http_from_alb" {
  security_group_id            = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
  description                  = "HTTP API from ALB"
  from_port                    = 8123
  to_port                      = 8123
  ip_protocol                  = "tcp"
  referenced_security_group_id = aws_security_group.alb.id
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "ch_http_self" {
  security_group_id            = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
  description                  = "HTTP API between nodes"
  from_port                    = 8123
  to_port                      = 8123
  ip_protocol                  = "tcp"
  referenced_security_group_id = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "ch_native_vpc" {
  security_group_id = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
  description       = "Native protocol from VPC"
  from_port         = 9000
  to_port           = 9000
  ip_protocol       = "tcp"
  cidr_ipv4         = var.vpc_cidr
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "ch_native_self" {
  security_group_id            = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
  description                  = "Native protocol between nodes"
  from_port                    = 9000
  to_port                      = 9000
  ip_protocol                  = "tcp"
  referenced_security_group_id = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "ch_interserver" {
  security_group_id            = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
  description                  = "Interserver replication"
  from_port                    = 9009
  to_port                      = 9009
  ip_protocol                  = "tcp"
  referenced_security_group_id = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "ch_metrics" {
  security_group_id = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
  description       = "Prometheus metrics"
  from_port         = 9363
  to_port           = 9363
  ip_protocol       = "tcp"
  cidr_ipv4         = var.vpc_cidr
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_egress_rule" "ch_all" {
  security_group_id = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
  ip_protocol       = "-1"
  cidr_ipv4         = "0.0.0.0/0"
}

# --- Keeper SG rules ---

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "keeper_from_ch" {
  security_group_id            = aws_security_group.keeper.id
  description                  = "Keeper client from ClickHouse"
  from_port                    = 9181
  to_port                      = 9181
  ip_protocol                  = "tcp"
  referenced_security_group_id = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "keeper_self" {
  security_group_id            = aws_security_group.keeper.id
  description                  = "Keeper client between nodes"
  from_port                    = 9181
  to_port                      = 9181
  ip_protocol                  = "tcp"
  referenced_security_group_id = aws_security_group.keeper.id
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "keeper_raft" {
  security_group_id            = aws_security_group.keeper.id
  description                  = "Keeper Raft consensus"
  from_port                    = 9234
  to_port                      = 9234
  ip_protocol                  = "tcp"
  referenced_security_group_id = aws_security_group.keeper.id
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_egress_rule" "keeper_all" {
  security_group_id = aws_security_group.keeper.id
  ip_protocol       = "-1"
  cidr_ipv4         = "0.0.0.0/0"
}

# --- ALB SG rules ---

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "alb_http" {
  security_group_id = aws_security_group.alb.id
  description       = "HTTP from VPC"
  from_port         = 80
  to_port           = 80
  ip_protocol       = "tcp"
  cidr_ipv4         = var.vpc_cidr
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_egress_rule" "alb_all" {
  security_group_id = aws_security_group.alb.id
  ip_protocol       = "-1"
  cidr_ipv4         = "0.0.0.0/0"
}

################################################################################
# CloudShell VPC Security Group
################################################################################

resource "aws_security_group" "cloudshell" {
  name_prefix = "${local.name_prefix}-cloudshell-"
  description = "Security group for CloudShell VPC environment"
  vpc_id      = module.vpc.vpc_id
  tags        = { Name = "${local.name_prefix}-cloudshell-sg" }

  lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true }
}

resource "aws_vpc_security_group_egress_rule" "cloudshell_all" {
  security_group_id = aws_security_group.cloudshell.id
  ip_protocol       = "-1"
  cidr_ipv4         = "0.0.0.0/0"
}

# Allow CloudShell to access ClickHouse HTTP API
resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "ch_http_from_cloudshell" {
  security_group_id            = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
  description                  = "HTTP API from CloudShell"
  from_port                    = 8123
  to_port                      = 8123
  ip_protocol                  = "tcp"
  referenced_security_group_id = aws_security_group.cloudshell.id
}

# Allow CloudShell to access ClickHouse native protocol
resource "aws_vpc_security_group_ingress_rule" "ch_native_from_cloudshell" {
  security_group_id            = aws_security_group.clickhouse.id
  description                  = "Native protocol from CloudShell"
  from_port                    = 9000
  to_port                      = 9000
  ip_protocol                  = "tcp"
  referenced_security_group_id = aws_security_group.cloudshell.id
}

追加しました。`networking.tf`にS3 Gateway Endpointを定義しています。

- ClickHouseのS3トラフィックがNAT Gatewayをバイパスし、AWSバックボーンを直接通る

feat: S3 Gateway Endpoint を追加

コスト削減とスループット改善が見込める。

EOF
)"

message.content: この場合、ECS taskが再起動したらs3_cacheは揮発しますか? また、cacheはすぐに復旧しますか?

content.text:
### S3キャッシュは揮発するか

**揮発しません**。ホストパスマウント（`/var/lib/clickhouse/s3cache` → EC2のNVMe）を使っているため、ECSタスクが再起動してもEC2ホスト上のキャッシュデータはそのまま残ります。

揮発するケース:
- **EC2インスタンス自体の停止/終了** — NVMeはインスタンスストアなのでデータ消失
- **別のEC2に再スケジュール** — ただしplacement constraintで固定ノードに配置しているため、通常は発生しない

### キャッシュ復旧の速度

キャッシュが消失した場合（EC2交換時など）:

| フェーズ | 挙動 |
|----------|------|
| 起動直後 | キャッシュは空。全クエリがS3から直接読み取り（レイテンシ100-200ms） |
| 徐々に復旧 | `cache_on_write_operations=1`により書き込みデータは即キャッシュ。読み取りデータもアクセス時にキャッシュされる |
| 定常状態 | ホットデータがキャッシュに載り、大半のクエリがNVMeから応答 |

**復旧速度はワークロード依存**です。キャッシュウォームアップを能動的に行いたい場合は、頻出クエリを意図的に実行してホットデータを先に載せることもできます。ただし、`do_not_evict_index_and_mark_files=1`が設定されているため、インデックス/マークファイルは一度キャッシュされれば追い出されず、クエリ性能の回復は比較的早いです。

message.content: EC2 insstanceの再起動などによってどのような影響がありますか? また、EC2の再起動やEC2のVM入れ替えはどの程度発生する可能性があるでしょうか

content.text:
### EC2再起動・入れ替え時の影響

| イベント | NVMeキャッシュ | Keeperデータ(EBS) | ClickHouseサービス |
|----------|---------------|-------------------|-------------------|
| **ECSタスク再起動** | 維持 | 維持 | 数秒のダウンタイム。レプリカがあるため読み取りは継続可能 |
| **EC2再起動(reboot)** | **維持** | 維持 | 1-2分。ECSタスクが再スケジュールされる |
| **EC2停止→起動 / 入れ替え** | **消失** | 維持 | キャッシュコールドスタート。S3直接読み取りで性能劣化 |

### EC2の再起動・入れ替えが発生するケース

#### 頻度: 低〜中（年数回程度）

| 原因 | 頻度 | 予測可能性 |
|------|------|-----------|
| **AWSメンテナンスイベント** | 年1-3回程度 | 事前通知あり（Personal Health Dashboard） |
| **ハードウェア障害** | 年0-1回程度 | 予測不可 |
| **Spot Instanceの中断** | — | 現構成はオンデマンドなので該当なし |
| **ASGのインスタンスリフレッシュ** | 手動トリガー時 | AMI更新時などに自分で実行 |
| **カーネルライブパッチ非対応の更新** | 年1-2回程度 | Amazon Linux自動更新設定に依存 |

#### AWSメンテナンスイベントの実態

AWSは基本的に**ライブマイグレーション**でホストを移行しますが、NVMe（インスタンスストア）付きインスタンスは**ライブマイグレーション非対応**です。r6gd.4xlargeはインスタンスストア付きなので、メンテナンス時は**停止→別ホストで起動**となり、NVMeデータは消失します。

### 現構成での緩和策

既に効いているもの:

1. **2レプリカ構成** — 1ノードがダウンしても他レプリカで読み書き継続
2. **S3プライマリストレージ** — データ本体はS3にあるため、NVMe消失でもデータロスなし
3. **`do_not_evict_index_and_mark_files=1`** — キャッシュ復旧時にインデックスが優先的に維持される
4. **Keeper EBS (`delete_on_termination=false`)** — EC2入れ替え後もクラスタ状態を維持

追加で検討できること:

- **メンテナンスウィンドウの監視** — Personal Health Dashboardのイベントをアラート通知し、事前にキャッシュウォームアップやレプリカ側へのトラフィック偏重を準備
- **ASG Lifecycle Hooks** — インスタンス終了前にレプリケーションキューの消化を待つ

ただし、キャッシュのコールドスタートは**性能劣化であってサービス停止ではない**ため、現構成で十分なリスク許容度であれば追加対応は不要です。

message.content: ASGでEC2が自動復旧するだけでは障害からの復旧としては問題ないでしょうか? clickhouse server, keeperについてそれぞれ考察してください

content.text:
重要な問題があります。特にKeeperのEBS永続化に設計上の欠陥があります。

## ClickHouse Server

### ASG自動復旧で概ね問題なし

| フェーズ | 挙動 | リスク |
|----------|------|--------|
| インスタンス障害検知 | ASGのヘルスチェック → 自動置換 | 検知まで数分のラグ |
| 新インスタンス起動 | user_data実行 → ECSエージェント登録 → タスクスケジュール | 3-5分程度 |
| ClickHouse起動 | Keeper接続 → ReplicatedMergeTreeの復旧 | レプリケーションキューのキャッチアップ |
| キャッシュ | NVMe消失 → S3直接読み取り → 徐々にウォームアップ | 性能劣化のみ、データロスなし |

**問題が少ない理由**:
- データ本体はS3にあり消失しない
- 2レプリカ構成のため、1ノードダウン中もう1つが読み書きを継続
- 復旧後はレプリケーションキューから差分をキャッチアップ

**注意点**:
- 同一シャードの2レプリカが同時に落ちるとそのシャードのデータが一時的に読めない（Distributedテーブル経由のクエリが部分的に失敗）
- キャッチアップ中のレプリケーションキューが大量に溜まっている場合、復旧に時間がかかる

---

## Keeper — 設計上の問題あり

### 現在のEBS永続化は実際には機能しない

現在の実装では`block_device_mappings`でEBSを定義しています:

```hcl
block_device_mappings {
  device_name = "/dev/xvdf"
  ebs {
    ...
    delete_on_termination = false
  }
}
```

**問題**: ASGがインスタンスを入れ替えると:

1. 旧インスタンスが終了 → `delete_on_termination = false`なのでEBSは**孤立して残る**
2. 新インスタンスが起動 → launch templateから**新しい空のEBS**が作成される
3. 旧EBSは再アタッチされない

つまり、**インスタンス入れ替え時にKeeperの状態は消失します**。

### Keeperの状態が消失した場合の影響

| シナリオ | 影響 |
|----------|------|
| 1ノード消失 | 残り2ノードでクォーラム維持。新ノードはリーダーからスナップショットを取得して復旧可能 |
| 2ノード同時消失 | **クォーラム喪失**。全ClickHouseのReplicatedMergeTreeテーブルがreadonlyになる |
| 3ノード同時消失 | **クラスタ状態の完全喪失**。手動でのメタデータ復旧が必要 |

### 1ノード消失なら自動復旧するが...

Keeperの Raftプロトコルにより、1ノードが空の状態で再参加してもリーダーからスナップショットを受け取って復旧できます。ただし:

- **復旧中のリスク窓**: 1ノードが復旧完了するまでの間、残り2ノードでギリギリのクォーラム。この間にもう1ノード落ちるとクォーラム喪失
- **スナップショット転送の負荷**: 大量のznodeがある場合、リーダーに負荷がかかる

### 推奨する修正

`block_device_mappings`ではなく、**独立したEBSボリュームを作成し、user_dataで再アタッチ**する方式に変更すべきです:

```
┌─ Terraform ─────────────────────┐
│ aws_ebs_volume (固定、3つ)       │
│ ↕ タグでKeeper node IDを識別    │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
        ↓
┌─ user_data ─────────────────────┐
│ 1. 自ノードIDのタグでEBSを検索   │
│ 2. aws ec2 attach-volume        │
│ 3. mount                        │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

修正しますか？

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Attach and mount the persistent EBS volume for Keeper data
VOLUME_ID="${ebs_volume_id}"
DEVICE="/dev/xvdf"
MOUNT_POINT="/mnt/keeper-data"
REGION=$(ec2-metadata --availability-zone | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's/.$//')
INSTANCE_ID=$(ec2-metadata --instance-id | awk '{print $2}')

# Wait for the volume to be available (may still be detaching from old instance)
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  STATE=$(aws ec2 describe-volumes --volume-ids "$VOLUME_ID" --region "$REGION" \
    --query 'Volumes[0].State' --output text)
  if [ "$STATE" = "available" ]; then
    break
  fi
  echo "Waiting for volume $VOLUME_ID to become available (state: $STATE)... ($i/60)"
  sleep 5
done

# Attach the volume
aws ec2 attach-volume --volume-id "$VOLUME_ID" --instance-id "$INSTANCE_ID" \
  --device "$DEVICE" --region "$REGION"

# Wait for the device to appear
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  if [ -b "$DEVICE" ]; then
    break
  fi
  echo "Waiting for device $DEVICE... ($i/30)"
  sleep 2
done

# Format only if not already formatted
if ! blkid "$DEVICE" &>/dev/null; then
  mkfs.ext4 -L keeper-data "$DEVICE"
fi

mkdir -p "$MOUNT_POINT"
mount -o noatime "$DEVICE" "$MOUNT_POINT"

# Ensure mount survives reboot
if ! grep -q "$MOUNT_POINT" /etc/fstab; then
  echo "LABEL=keeper-data $MOUNT_POINT ext4 defaults,noatime 0 2" >> /etc/fstab
fi

# Configure ECS agent
cat <<EOF >> /etc/ecs/ecs.config
ECS_CLUSTER=${cluster_name}
ECS_INSTANCE_ATTRIBUTES={"${node_attribute}":"${node_value}"}
ECS_ENABLE_CONTAINER_METADATA=true
EOF

content.text: IAMポリシーのconditionを確認します。`ec2:AttachVolume`と`ec2:DetachVolume`はボリュームとインスタンス両方のARNに対する権限が必要で、conditionのタグフィルタリングが`DescribeVolumes`と組み合わせでうまく動くか確認します。
`common_tags`に`Project`タグが含まれており、EBSボリュームにも付与されるので条件は機能します。ただしAttach/Detach時はインスタンスのARNにも権限が必要なので、conditionをボリューム操作のみに限定してIAMポリシーを修正します。

content.text:
修正完了です。変更内容のまとめ:

### 変更点

**`ecs_cluster.tf`**
- `block_device_mappings`をlaunch templateから削除
- 独立した`aws_ebs_volume.keeper`を`for_each`で3つ作成（`prevent_destroy = true`で誤削除防止）
- EBSのvolume IDをuser_dataにテンプレート変数として渡す

**`templates/keeper_user_data.sh`**
- `ec2-metadata`でインスタンスID・リージョンを取得
- `aws ec2 describe-volumes`でボリュームが`available`になるまで待機（旧インスタンスからのデタッチ完了を待つ）
- `aws ec2 attach-volume`で自インスタンスにアタッチ
- デバイス出現を待ってからフォーマット（初回のみ）・マウント

**`iam.tf`**
- EC2インスタンスロールに`ec2:AttachVolume`/`ec2:DetachVolume`/`ec2:DescribeVolumes`権限を追加
- `Project`タグ条件でスコープを限定

### インスタンス入れ替え時の動作

```
旧EC2終了 → EBSが自動デタッチ(available状態)
    ↓
新EC2起動 → user_dataがEBSのavailableを待機
    ↓
aws ec2 attach-volume → マウント → ECSエージェント起動
    ↓
Keeperがcoordination dataを読み込み → 状態復元完了
```

message.content: これが実現されれば耐障害性は優れていますか?

content.text:
1ノード障害であれば十分な耐障害性があります。ただし、考慮すべきリスク窓が残っています。

### ClickHouse Server

| シナリオ | 耐障害性 | 理由 |
|----------|----------|------|
| 1ノード障害 | **問題なし** | 同一シャードのもう1レプリカが継続。復旧後にキャッチアップ |
| 同一シャードの2レプリカ同時障害 | **部分停止** | そのシャードのデータが一時的に読めない。別シャードは正常 |
| 全4ノード同時障害 | **全停止** | S3にデータはあるので復旧後にデータロスなし |

データロスのリスクは実質ゼロ（S3が永続ストレージ）。問題は可用性のみ。

### Keeper

| シナリオ | 耐障害性 | 理由 |
|----------|----------|------|
| 1ノード障害 | **問題なし** | 2/3でクォーラム維持。EBSから状態復元 |
| 2ノード同時障害 | **クォーラム喪失** | ClickHouseの全ReplicatedMergeTreeテーブルがreadonlyになる |
| 3ノード同時障害 | **全停止** | EBSが残っていれば3ノード復旧後に復元可能 |

### 残るリスク窓

**1ノード復旧中にもう1ノードが落ちる（Keeper）**

```
Keeper-1障害 → ASG置換開始（3-5分）
    ↓ この間、Keeper-2 + Keeper-3 の2ノードでギリギリのクォーラム
    ↓ ここでKeeper-2が落ちるとクォーラム喪失
Keeper-1復旧完了
```

この窓は現構成では避けられません。緩和策として:

| 対策 | 効果 | コスト |
|------|------|--------|
| **Keeperを5ノードに増やす** | 2ノード同時障害に耐えられる | EC2 2台追加 |
| **Multi-AZでのAZ分散** | AZ障害で最大1ノードしか失わない | 既に実現済み（3AZ） |
| **ASGの復旧速度向上** | リスク窓を短縮 | Warm poolの設定 |

### 結論

現構成（3 Keeper + 2×2 ClickHouse + EBS永続化 + 3AZ分散）は、**単一ノード障害に対しては十分な耐障害性**があります。AZ分散により、単一AZ障害でもKeeperは2/3、ClickHouseは各シャード1レプリカが生存します。

2ノード同時障害への耐性が必要かどうかは、SLAの要件次第です。ログ基盤という用途を考えると、短時間のreadonly状態が許容できるなら現構成で十分と考えます。

message.content: ASGで単一のAZでEC2が起動しない場合は別のNodeに起動するみたいなスケジューリングの工夫はできませんか

content.text:
EBSがAZに紐づくため、そのままでは別AZにフェイルオーバーできません。ただし工夫すれば可能です。

### 現状の制約

```
EBS (ap-northeast-1a) ←→ EC2 (ap-northeast-1a) のみアタッチ可能
                        ✗ EC2 (ap-northeast-1c) にはアタッチ不可
```

ASGの`vpc_zone_identifier`を複数AZにしても、EBSがある AZでしかデータを復元できません。

### 解決策の比較

| 方式 | AZフェイルオーバー | データ鮮度 | 複雑度 | レイテンシ |
|------|-------------------|-----------|--------|-----------|
| **A. EBSスナップショット + 別AZ復元** | 可能 | スナップショット間隔分のラグ | 中 | EBS相当 |
| **B. EFS** | 可能（マルチAZ標準） | リアルタイム | 低 | EBSより高い |
| **C. 現状維持（EBS固定AZ）** | 不可 | リアルタイム | 低 | 最低 |

### 推奨: 方式A（EBSスナップショット）

Keeperはディスクレイテンシに敏感なため、EFSよりEBSが適しています。DLM（Data Lifecycle Manager）で定期スナップショットを取り、別AZ起動時にスナップショットから復元する方式が最もバランスが良いです。

```
通常時:
  EC2(1a) ←→ EBS(1a)  ── DLMで1時間ごとにスナップショット

AZ障害時:
  1a障害 → ASGが1cでEC2起動
         → user_dataが最新スナップショットからEBS(1c)を作成
         → アタッチ・マウント
         → Keeperがスナップショット時点の状態から復旧
         → Raftでリーダーから差分キャッチアップ
```

スナップショット間隔分のデータラグがありますが、Keeperの Raftプロトコルがリーダーから差分を補完するため、実質的なデータロスはありません。

修正しますか？

  +REGION=$(ec2-metadata --availability-zone | awk '{print $2}' | sed 's/.$//')

  +  echo "Waiting for volume $VOLUME_ID to become available (state: $STATE)... ($i/60)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: Keeper EBS永続化をインスタンス入れ替えに対応" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Keeper EBSの永続化方式を`block_device_mappings`から独立`aws_ebs_volume`に変更
- ASGのインスタンス入れ替え時に旧EBSを再アタッチし、Keeperの状態を復元可能に
- EC2インスタンスロールにEBSアタッチ権限を追加（Projectタグで最小権限）

### 背景

`block_device_mappings`で定義したEBSは、ASGがインスタンスを入れ替えると新しい空のボリュームが作成され、旧ボリュームは孤立して残る。そのため、インスタンス障害時にKeeperの coordination dataが消失し、Raftリーダーからのスナップショット転送に依存していた。

### 変更後の動作

```
旧EC2終了 → EBSが自動デタッチ(available状態)
新EC2起動 → user_dataがEBSのavailableを待機
          → aws ec2 attach-volume → マウント → ECSエージェント起動
          → Keeperがcoordination dataを読み込み → 状態復元完了
```

## Test plan

- [ ] `terraform plan`で`aws_ebs_volume`の作成と`block_device_mappings`の削除を確認
- [ ] Keeper EC2を手動終了し、ASGが新インスタンスを起動した際にEBSが再アタッチされることを確認
- [ ] 再アタッチ後、`echo "mntr" | nc keeper-X.logplatform.local 9181 | grep zk_znode_count`でznode数が維持されていることを確認
- [ ] IAMポリシーがProjectタグ条件で正しくスコープされていることを確認
EOF
)"

message.content: 障害復旧とは別にバージョンアップ作業はできますか?

content.text:
はい、可能です。現構成でのバージョンアップ方法を整理します。

### ローリングアップデート（無停止）

ClickHouseは異なるバージョンのノードが一時的に混在するクラスタをサポートしています。

**Keeper（先にアップグレード）**:

```
1. Keeper-1のECRイメージを新バージョンに更新
2. ECSタスク定義を更新 → 新タスクデプロイ
3. Keeper-1復帰確認（echo ruok | nc ...）
4. Keeper-2, Keeper-3を順次同様に実施
```

Raftクォーラム（2/3）を維持するため、**1ノードずつ**実施。

**ClickHouse Server（Keeper後にアップグレード）**:

```
1. shard1-replica1のECRイメージを更新 → ECSタスク再デプロイ
2. レプリケーションキュー消化を確認
   SELECT count() FROM system.replication_queue
3. shard1-replica2 → shard2-replica1 → shard2-replica2 の順に実施
```

同一シャード内の**1レプリカずつ**実施し、もう1レプリカで読み書きを継続。

### 現構成での実施方法

ECSタスク定義のイメージタグを更新して`aws ecs update-service --force-new-deployment`で1ノードずつ実施することになりますが、**現状のTerraformでは全ノードが同一イメージタグ（`latest`）を参照している**ため、ローリングアップデートの制御が難しいです。

```hcl
# 現状: 全ノードが同じイメージ
image = "${aws_ecr_repository.repos["clickhouse-keeper"].repository_url}:latest"
```

### 改善案

ローリングアップデートを安全に行うには:

| 方式 | 概要 | 複雑度 |
|------|------|--------|
| **A. イメージタグのバージョン固定** | `latest`→`24.12`等に変更。Terraform変数でバージョン管理 | 低 |
| **B. デプロイスクリプト** | 1ノードずつタスク定義更新→デプロイ→ヘルスチェック待機を自動化 | 中 |
| **C. A + B の組み合わせ** | Terraformでバージョン管理 + スクリプトでローリング制御 | 中 |

方式Aは最低限必要です。`latest`のままだと、どのノードがどのバージョンで動いているか追跡できず、意図しないアップグレードも起こり得ます。

対応しますか？

message.content: 対応してください。バージョンアップの検証のためにdockerhubを確認して最新の一つ前のバージョン (minorで可能) を指定してください。

The content doesn't include a list of available Docker image tags matching the pattern X.Y-alpine (such as 24.12-alpine, 24.11-alpine, etc.).

To find the latest 5 minor version tags for clickhouse/clickhouse-keeper with the -alpine suffix, you would need to:

1. Visit the Docker Hub repository directly at: `docker.io/library/clickhouse/clickhouse-keeper`
2. Navigate to the "Tags" section
3. Filter or search for tags ending in "-alpine"
4. Sort by version in descending order

Alternatively, you could use the Docker CLI command:
```
docker search clickhouse/clickhouse-keeper
```

Or check the official ClickHouse repository documentation for available image tags.

/usr/bin/curl -s "https://registry.hub.docker.com/v2/repositories/clickhouse/clickhouse-server/tags?page_size=100&name=alpine" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
tags = []
for r in data['results']:
    name = r['name']
    parts = name.replace('-alpine','').split('.')
    if name.endswith('-alpine') and len(parts) == 2 and parts[0].isdigit() and parts[1].isdigit():
        tags.append((int(parts[0]), int(parts[1]), name))
tags.sort(reverse=True)
for _, _, name in tags[:10]:
    print(name)
"

content.text:
現状を整理します:

- **Dockerfile**: ベースイメージに`24.12-alpine`をハードコード
- **ECSタスク定義**: ECRリポジトリの`latest`タグを参照

バージョン管理を以下の方針で修正します:
1. Dockerfileで`ARG`によるバージョン指定

toolUseResult.originalFile:
FROM clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.12-alpine

# Remove docker_related_config to prevent listen_host conflicts
RUN rm -f /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/docker_related_config.xml

# Override configs go to config.d/ (default config.xml is preserved)
COPY config/config.xml /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/custom.xml
COPY config/remote_servers.xml /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/remote_servers.xml
COPY config/macros.xml /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/macros.xml
COPY config/keeper.xml /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/keeper.xml

# listen_host config
COPY config/listen.xml /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/listen.xml

# Restore console logging (removed with docker_related_config.xml)
COPY config/logger.xml /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/logger.xml

# Users config override (placeholders replaced at runtime by entrypoint-wrapper.sh)
COPY config/users.xml /etc/clickhouse-server/users.d/custom.xml

# Custom entrypoint that substitutes env vars into users config
COPY entrypoint-wrapper.sh /entrypoint-wrapper.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint-wrapper.sh

# Schema files are NOT in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/
# Apply schema manually after cluster is running (see README)

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint-wrapper.sh"]

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8123/ping || exit 1

EXPOSE 8123 9000 9009 9363

gh pr create --title "chore: ClickHouseイメージバージョンを変数化" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- `latest`タグをやめ、Terraform変数でイメージバージョンを明示管理する仕組みを導入
- DockerfileにARGを追加し、ビルド時にバージョン指定可能に
- バージョンアップのローリングアップデート検証のため、現行`24.12`から一つ前のminor `24.11-alpine`を設定

### 変更内容

| ファイル | 変更 |
|----------|------|
| `Dockerfile` / `Dockerfile.keeper` | `ARG CLICKHOUSE_VERSION`でベースイメージを可変化 |
| `variables.tf` | `clickhouse_image_tag` / `keeper_image_tag`変数を追加（デフォルト: `24.11-alpine`） |
| `ecs_clickhouse.tf` / `ecs_keeper.tf` | イメージタグを`latest`→`var.*_image_tag`に変更 |

### バージョンアップ手順（ローリングアップデート）

```bash
# 1. Keeper（1ノードずつ）
terraform apply -var keeper_image_tag="24.12-alpine" -target=aws_ecs_task_definition.keeper[\"1\"]
aws ecs update-service --force-new-deployment ...
# → 復帰確認後、ノード2, 3を順次実施

# 2. ClickHouse Server（同一シャード内1レプリカずつ）
terraform apply -var clickhouse_image_tag="24.12-alpine" -target=aws_ecs_task_definition.clickhouse[\"s1r1\"]
aws ecs update-service --force-new-deployment ...
# → レプリケーションキュー消化確認後、次のレプリカへ
```

## Test plan

- [ ] `terraform plan`でイメージタグが`24.11-alpine`に変更されることを確認
- [ ] ECRに`24.11-alpine`タグのイメージをpushし、ECSタスクが正常に起動することを確認
- [ ] `curl -sf "http://${CH_HOST}:8123" --user "default:${CH_PASSWORD}" --data "SELECT version()"`でバージョンを確認
- [ ] 変数を`24.12-alpine`に変更して再度`terraform plan`し、タスク定義のみが差分になることを確認
EOF
)"

content.content:
{"body":"## Summary

- Keeper EBSの永続化方式を`block_device_mappings`から独立`aws_ebs_volume`に変更
- ASGのインスタンス入れ替え時に旧EBSを再アタッチし、Keeperの状態を復元可能に
- EC2インスタンスロールにEBSアタッチ権限を追加（Projectタグで最小権限）

### 背景

`block_device_mappings`で定義したEBSは、ASGがインスタンスを入れ替えると新しい空のボリュームが作成され、旧ボリュームは孤立して残る。そのため、インスタンス障害時にKeeperの coordination dataが消失し、Raftリーダーからのスナップショット転送に依存していた。

### 変更後の動作

```
旧EC2終了 → EBSが自動デタッチ(available状態)
新EC2起動 → user_dataがEBSのavailableを待機
          → aws ec2 attach-volume → マウント → ECSエージェント起動
          → Keeperがcoordination dataを読み込み → 状態復元完了
```

## Test plan

- [x] `terraform plan`で`aws_ebs_volume`の作成と`block_device_mappings`の削除を確認
- [ ] Keeper EC2を手動終了し、ASGが新インスタンスを起動した際にEBSが再アタッチされることを確認
- [ ] 再アタッチ後、`echo "mntr" | nc keeper-X.logplatform.local 9181 | grep zk_znode_count`でznode数が維持されていることを確認
- [ ] IAMポリシーがProjectタグ条件で正しくスコープされていることを確認","commits":[{"authoredDate":"2026-04-05T00:44:33Z","authors":[{"email":"nthouse14@gmail.com","id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMyOTYyNDkz","login":"taxintt","name":"taxintt"}],"committedDate":"2026-04-05T00:44:33Z","messageBody":"block_device_mappingsで定義したEBSはASGのインスタンス入れ替え時に
新しい空のボリュームが作成され、旧ボリュームは孤立して残るため、
実際にはKeeperの状態が永続化されていなかった。

独立したaws_ebs_volumeを作成し、user_dataでaws ec2 attach-volume
により再アタッチする方式に変更:
- aws_ebs_volume: prevent_destroy=trueで誤削除防止
- user_data: ボリュームのavailable待機→アタッチ→マウント
- IAM: EC2インスタンスロールにEBSアタッチ権限を追加（Projectタグで限定）

content.text:
## EBS再アタッチ検証手順

### 前提

`terraform apply`済みで、PR #7 の変更が反映されていること。

### Step 1: 現在のKeeperの状態を記録

```bash
# 対象ノード（例: keeper-2）のznode数を記録
echo "mntr" | nc keeper-2.logplatform.local 9181 | grep zk_znode_count
# → zk_znode_count  193  ← この数値をメモ

# EBSのボリュームIDとアタッチ状態を確認
aws ec2 describe-volumes \
  --filters "Name=tag:Name,Values=*keeper-2*" \
  --query 'Volumes[].{ID:VolumeId,State:State,AZ:AvailabilityZone,AttachedTo:Attachments[0].InstanceId}' \
  --output table
```

### Step 2: Keeper-2のEC2インスタンスを特定して終了

```bash
# keeper-2が動作しているEC2インスタンスIDを特定
INSTANCE_ID=$(aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --filters "Name=tag:KeeperNode,Values=2" "Name=instance-state-name,Values=running" \
  --query 'Reservations[].Instances[].InstanceId' --output text)
echo "Terminating: $INSTANCE_ID"

# インスタンスを終了（ASGのスケールイン保護を一時解除）
aws autoscaling set-instance-protection \
  --instance-ids "$INSTANCE_ID" \
  --auto-scaling-group-name "$(terraform output -raw name_prefix)-keeper-2" \
  --no-protected-from-scale-in

aws ec2 terminate-instances --instance-ids "$INSTANCE_ID"
```

### Step 3: EBSのデタッチ→再アタッチを監視

```bash
# EBSの状態遷移を監視（in-use → available → in-use になれば成功）
watch -n 5 'aws ec2 describe-volumes \
  --filters "Name=tag:Name,Values=*keeper-2*" \
  --query "Volumes[].{State:State,AttachedTo:Attachments[0].InstanceId}" \
  --output table'
```

期待する遷移:
```
in-use (旧インスタンス)
  → available (デタッチ完了)
  → in-use (新インスタンスに再アタッチ完了)
```

### Step 4: 新インスタンスの起動を確認

```bash
# ASGが新インスタンスを起動するまで待機（3-5分）
watch -n 10 'aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --filters "Name=tag:KeeperNode,Values=2" "Name=instance-state-name,Values=running" \
  --query "Reservations[].Instances[].{ID:InstanceId,State:State.Name,LaunchTime:LaunchTime}" \
  --output table'
```

### Step 5: Keeper復帰とznode数の確認

```bash
# Keeperがヘルスチェックに応答するまで待機
until echo "ruok" | nc keeper-2.logplatform.local 9181 | grep -q imok; do
  echo "Waiting for keeper-2..."
  sleep 10
done
echo "keeper-2 is back!"

# znode数を確認（Step 1でメモした値と一致すれば成功）
echo "mntr" | nc keeper-2.logplatform.local 9181 | grep zk_znode_count

# クラスタ全体の状態確認
for i in 1 2 3; do
  echo "--- keeper-$i ---"
  echo "stat" | nc keeper-$i.logplatform.local 9181 | grep -E "Mode|Node count"
done
```

### Step 6: ClickHouseからの接続確認

```bash
# ReplicatedMergeTreeテーブルがreadonlyになっていないことを確認
curl -sf "http://${CH_HOST}:8123" \
  --user "default:${CH_PASSWORD}" \
  --data "SELECT database, table, is_readonly FROM system.replicas FORMAT PrettyCompact"
# → 全行 is_readonly = 0 なら正常
```

### 判定基準

| チェック項目 | 成功条件 |
|-------------|---------|
| EBSが新インスタンスに再アタッチ | `describe-volumes`でState=in-use、新InstanceIdにアタッチ |
| znode数の維持 | 再起動前後で`zk_znode_count`が同値 |
| Keeperクォーラム | 3ノード全てが`ruok`→`imok`を返す |
| ClickHouse | `is_readonly = 0`（全レプリカ） |

### トラブルシューティング

EBSがアタッチされない場合:

```bash
# user_dataのログを確認（SSM Session Manager経由）
aws ssm start-session --target <新インスタンスID>
# セッション内で:
sudo cat /var/log/cloud-init-output.log | grep -A 5 "attach-volume"
```

content.text:
**削除すべきです**。

このPRにより`do_not_evict_index_and_mark_files`は**23.7で削除**されています。設定自体が「パフォーマンスを悪化させるだけだった」というBackward Incompatible Changeです。

現在使用しているバージョン（24.12/24.11）では設定が無視されるか、起動時に警告が出ている可能性があります。

    <merge_tree>
        <allow_remote_fs_zero_copy_replication>0</allow_remote_fs_zero_copy_replication>
    </merge_tree>

    <prometheus>
        <endpoint>/metrics</endpoint>
        <port>9363</port>
        <metrics>1</metrics>
        <events>1</events>
        <asynchronous_metrics>1</asynchronous_metrics>
    </prometheus>
</clickhouse>

input.prompt: Extract all configuration options for the filesystem cache (type: cache) in ClickHouse. Focus on settings available in version 24.x. List each setting name, description, default value, and whether it's recommended. I'm especially looking for settings related to index/mark file caching, cache eviction policy, and any settings that replaced do_not_evict_index_and_mark_files.

content.content:
# ClickHouse Filesystem Cache Configuration Settings

Based on the documentation provided, here are the filesystem cache (type: cache) configuration options for ClickHouse 24.x:

## Disk Configuration Settings

| Setting | Description | Default | Recommended |
|---------|-------------|---------|-------------|
| `path` | "**Required**. Path to the directory where cache will be stored." | — | Yes |
| `max_size` | "**Required**. Maximum cache size in bytes or readable format (e.g., `10Gi`)." | — | Yes |
| `cache_on_write_operations` | Enables write-through caching for INSERT queries and background merges | `false` | Consider enabling |
| `enable_filesystem_query_cache_limit` | Activates per-query cache size limits based on `max_query_cache_size` | `false` | Optional |
| `enable_cache_hits_threshold` | "When enabled, data is cached only after being read multiple times." | `false` | Optional |
| `cache_hits_threshold` | Number of reads required before caching data | `0` | Depends on use case |
| `enable_bypass_cache_with_threshold` | Skips cache for large read operations | `false` | Optional |
| `bypass_cache_threshold` | "Read range size that triggers cache bypass" | `256Mi` | Tunable |
| `max_file_segment_size` | "Maximum size of a single cache file in bytes or readable format." | `8Mi` | Tunable |
| `max_elements` | Maximum number of cache files | `10000000` | Tunable |
| `load_metadata_threads` | Threads for loading cache metadata at startup | `16` | Tunable |
| `use_split_cache` | Separates files to system/data categories | `false` | Optional |
| `split_cache_ratio` | Ratio of system to total cache size | `0.1` | Optional |

## Query/Profile Settings

| Setting | Description | Default |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `enable_filesystem_cache` | "Enables/disables cache usage per query" | `true` |
| `read_from_filesystem_cache_if_exists_otherwise_bypass_cache` | Uses cache only if data exists; prevents new caching | `false` |
| `enable_filesystem_cache_on_write_operations` | Enables write-through cache (requires config option) | `false` (Cloud: `true`) |
| `enable_filesystem_cache_log` | "Enables detailed cache usage logging to `system.filesystem_cache_log`." | `false` |
| `filesystem_cache_allow_background_download` | Allows partial segments to finish in background | `true` |
| `max_query_cache_size` | Maximum per-query cache allocation | `false` |
| `filesystem_cache_skip_download_if_exceeds_per_query_cache_write_limit` | Controls eviction when query limit reached | `true` |

## Important Notes

The documentation does **not mention** `do_not_evict_index_and_mark_files` or any direct replacement for it in version 24.x. The cache uses "**LRU** cache policy" for eviction. For index/mark file caching specifically, no dedicated settings appear in the provided documentation—the cache treats all file types uniformly under the LRU eviction strategy.

content.text:
現在の設定で十分です。特に追加で設定する必要はありません。

`do_not_evict_index_and_mark_files`の直接的な後継設定はなく、24.x系ではLRUキャッシュが全ファイルタイプを均等に扱います。

ただし、現在のS3キャッシュ設定で**検討の余地がある設定**が1つあります:

| 設定 | 現状 | 検討 |
|------|------|------|
| `enable_bypass_cache_with_threshold` | 未設定（false） | 大きなフルスキャンクエリがキャッシュを汚染するのを防ぐ。ログ基盤で大量の`SELECT *`が走る場合に有効 |

ただしこれはチューニングの範疇で、現状のワークロードで問題が出てから対応すれば十分です。今の`max_size` + `cache_on_write_operations=1`の構成で問題ありません。

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive overview. Let me create a summary report of all the deployment scripts, shell scripts, and ops-related files found.

## Summary Report: Deployment Scripts, Shell Scripts, and Ops-Related Files

I've found a comprehensive set of deployment and operations scripts in your ClickHouse ECS sample repository. Here's a complete inventory:

### **Core Deployment & Operations Scripts**

#### 1. **Main Deployment Script**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/infra/scripts/deploy.sh`
- **Purpose:** Primary deployment orchestrator with ECS/EKS abstraction layer
- **Features:**
  - Deploys ClickHouse and Keeper services
  - Supports both ECS and EKS targets (configurable via `DEPLOY_TARGET` environment variable)
  - Manages 4 ClickHouse services (s1r1, s1r2, s2r1, s2r2) and 3 Keeper services
  - Force-new-deployment and rollout status tracking
  - Configurable via environment variables: `PROJECT`, `ENVIRONMENT`, `AWS_REGION`

#### 2. **Health Check Script**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/infra/scripts/health-check.sh`
- **Purpose:** Comprehensive ClickHouse cluster health monitoring
- **Checks:**
  - Basic ping/connectivity
  - Cluster status from system.clusters
  - Replication queue size
  - Keeper connectivity via system.zookeeper
  - Storage disk status
  - Exit codes for failure detection

### **Infrastructure User Data Scripts**

#### 3. **ClickHouse User Data Script**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/infra/terraform/templates/clickhouse_user_data.sh`
- **Purpose:** EC2 instance initialization for ClickHouse nodes
- **Tasks:**
  - Configures ECS agent with cluster and node attributes
  - Detects and mounts NVMe instance storage for S3 cache
  - Sets up proper permissions and filesystem configuration

#### 4. **Keeper User Data Script**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/infra/terraform/templates/keeper_user_data.sh`
- **Purpose:** EC2 instance initialization for ClickHouse Keeper coordination service
- **Tasks:**
  - Attaches and mounts persistent EBS volumes
  - Handles device polling and filesystem formatting
  - Configures automatic mount on reboot
  - Configures ECS agent with cluster and node attributes

#### 5. **ClickHouse Entrypoint Wrapper**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/infra/clickhouse/entrypoint-wrapper.sh`
- **Purpose:** Docker container startup script
- **Tasks:**
  - Validates required environment variables (CH_DEFAULT_PASSWORD, CH_READONLY_PASSWORD)
  - Injects passwords into ClickHouse users.d/custom.xml configuration
  - Handles XML escaping for sed replacements
  - Waits for Keeper nodes (3 hosts) to become reachable on port 9181
  - Creates S3 cache directory if NVMe not mounted

### **Benchmark & Data Loading Scripts**

#### 6. **Run Benchmark Script**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/benchmark/run_benchmark.sh`
- **Purpose:** MgBench SELECT query performance benchmarking
- **Features:**
  - 1 cold run + 3 warm runs per query
  - Cache dropping between cold runs
  - Parses queries from SQL file with metadata
  - Outputs TSV results with timing analysis
  - Generates summary statistics (cold, warm_min, warm_avg, warm_max)

#### 7. **Run Insert Benchmark Script**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/benchmark/run_insert_benchmark.sh`
- **Purpose:** INSERT performance benchmarking
- **Tests:**
  - A) Batch INSERT with varying sizes (1M, 10M, 100M rows)
  - B) HTTP streaming INSERT via curl pipe
  - Measures throughput (rows/sec)
  - Outputs TSV results with row counts and timing

#### 8. **Run Fulltext Benchmark Script**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/benchmark/run_fulltext_benchmark.sh`
- **Purpose:** Full-text search performance testing
- **Features:**
  - Tests 3 index types: noidx, tokenbf, ngrambf
  - 1 cold + 3 warm runs per query
  - Outputs per-index statistics
  - Summary table with warm average timings

#### 9. **Load Data Script**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/benchmark/data/load_data.sh`
- **Purpose:** Load MgBench datasets from public sources
- **Features:**
  - Loads 3 datasets: logs1, logs2, logs3
  - Uses ClickHouse url() table function
  - Tracks load times and row counts
  - Verifies replication across cluster

#### 10. **Amplify Data Script**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/benchmark/data/amplify_data.sh`
- **Purpose:** Scale test datasets to hundreds of millions of rows
- **Features:**
  - Iteratively doubles data while shifting timestamps
  - Target row counts configurable per table
  - Waits for replication between iterations
  - Runs OPTIMIZE TABLE FINAL for compression
  - Configurable targets: logs1 (300M), logs2 (500M), logs3 (300M default)

#### 11. **Populate Extended Script**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/benchmark/data/populate_extended.sh`
- **Purpose:** Generate extended logs2 tables with raw_log column
- **Features:**
  - Generates realistic log strings with structured data
  - Creates 3 table variants (noidx, tokenbf, ngrambf) for index testing
  - Includes request IDs, service names, error details
  - Tests fulltext search capabilities

### **Terraform Outputs**

#### 12. **Terraform Outputs Configuration**
- **Path:** `/Users/taxin/src/github.com/taxintt/clickhouse-ecs-sample/infra/terraform/outputs.tf`
- **Exported Values:**
  - `vpc_id`: VPC ID for the deployment
  - `private_subnet_ids`: Private subnet IDs
  - `ecs_cluster_name`: ECS cluster name
  - `ecr_repository_urls`: Docker image repository URLs (map)
  - `s3_data_bucket_name`: S3 bucket for data storage
  - `s3_data_bucket_endpoint`: S3 endpoint URL
  - `kinesis_stream_name`: Kinesis stream name for logs
  - `alb_dns_name`: Application Load Balancer DNS
  - `cloudshell_security_group_id`: Security group for CloudShell access
  - `clickhouse_fqdns`: ClickHouse service discovery FQDNs
  - `keeper_fqdns`: Keeper service discovery FQDNs

### **Usage Examples**

**Deploy all services:**
```bash
DEPLOY_TARGET=ecs ./deploy.sh all
```

**Health check:**
```bash
./health-check.sh clickhouse-host password
```

**Run benchmarks:**
```bash
./run_benchmark.sh --host clickhouse-host --password mypass --tag baseline
./run_insert_benchmark.sh --host clickhouse-host --password mypass
```

**Load and amplify data:**
```bash
./load_data.sh --host clickhouse-host --password mypass
./amplify_data.sh --host clickhouse-host --password mypass
```

This setup provides a complete operations pipeline from infrastructure provisioning through deployment, health monitoring, and comprehensive performance benchmarking.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	################################################################################
5	# Deploy script with ECS/EKS abstraction layer
6	# Usage: DEPLOY_TARGET=ecs ./deploy.sh [service_name]
7	# Examples:
8	#   DEPLOY_TARGET=ecs ./deploy.sh clickhouse
9	#   DEPLOY_TARGET=ecs ./deploy.sh keeper
10	#   DEPLOY_TARGET=ecs ./deploy.sh all
11	################################################################################
12	
13	DEPLOY_TARGET="${DEPLOY_TARGET:-ecs}"
14	SERVICE_NAME="${1:-all}"
15	PROJECT="${PROJECT:-logplatform}"
16	ENVIRONMENT="${ENVIRONMENT:-dev}"
17	AWS_REGION="${AWS_REGION:-ap-northeast-1}"
18	
19	CLUSTER_NAME="${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}"
20	
21	# ClickHouse and Keeper service names
22	CLICKHOUSE_SERVICES=(
23	  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-ch-s1r1"
24	  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-ch-s1r2"
25	  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-ch-s2r1"
26	  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-ch-s2r2"
27	)
28	KEEPER_SERVICES=(
29	  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-keeper-1"
30	  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-keeper-2"
31	  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-keeper-3"
32	)
33	
34	################################################################################
35	# Abstraction functions
36	################################################################################
37	
38	deploy_ecs_service() {
39	  local service_name="$1"
40	  echo "Deploying ECS service: ${service_name}"
41	  aws ecs update-service \
42	    --cluster "${CLUSTER_NAME}" \
43	    --service "${service_name}" \
44	    --force-new-deployment \
45	    --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
46	    --no-cli-pager
47	}
48	
49	deploy_eks_service() {
50	  local service_name="$1"
51	  echo "Deploying EKS service: ${service_name}"
52	  kubectl rollout restart "statefulset/${service_name}" -n "${PROJECT}"
53	}
54	
55	wait_ecs_stable() {
56	  local service_name="$1"
57	  echo "Waiting for ECS service to stabilize: ${service_name}"
58	  aws ecs wait services-stable \
59	    --cluster "${CLUSTER_NAME}" \
60	    --services "${service_name}" \
61	    --region "${AWS_REGION}"
62	}
63	
64	wait_eks_stable() {
65	  local service_name="$1"
66	  echo "Waiting for EKS rollout: ${service_name}"
67	  kubectl rollout status "statefulset/${service_name}" -n "${PROJECT}" --timeout=300s
68	}
69	
70	deploy_service() {
71	  local service_name="$1"
72	  case "${DEPLOY_TARGET}" in
73	    ecs) deploy_ecs_service "${service_name}" ;;
74	    eks) deploy_eks_service "${service_name}" ;;
75	    *)   echo "ERROR: Unknown DEPLOY_TARGET: ${DEPLOY_TARGET}"; exit 1 ;;
76	  esac
77	}
78	
79	wait_stable() {
80	  local service_name="$1"
81	  case "${DEPLOY_TARGET}" in
82	    ecs) wait_ecs_stable "${service_name}" ;;
83	    eks) wait_eks_stable "${service_name}" ;;
84	  esac
85	}
86	
87	################################################################################
88	# Deploy logic
89	################################################################################
90	
91	deploy_keeper() {
92	  echo "=== Deploying Keeper services ==="
93	  for svc in "${KEEPER_SERVICES[@]}"; do
94	    deploy_service "${svc}"
95	  done
96	  for svc in "${KEEPER_SERVICES[@]}"; do
97	    wait_stable "${svc}"
98	  done
99	  echo "=== Keeper deployment complete ==="
100	}
101	
102	deploy_clickhouse() {
103	  echo "=== Deploying ClickHouse services ==="
104	  for svc in "${CLICKHOUSE_SERVICES[@]}"; do
105	    deploy_service "${svc}"
106	  done
107	  for svc in "${CLICKHOUSE_SERVICES[@]}"; do
108	    wait_stable "${svc}"
109	  done
110	  echo "=== ClickHouse deployment complete ==="
111	}
112	
113	case "${SERVICE_NAME}" in
114	  keeper)
115	    deploy_keeper
116	    ;;
117	  clickhouse)
118	    deploy_clickhouse
119	    ;;
120	  all)
121	    deploy_keeper
122	    deploy_clickhouse
123	    ;;
124	  *)
125	    echo "ERROR: Unknown service: ${SERVICE_NAME}"
126	    echo "Usage: $0 [keeper|clickhouse|all]"
127	    exit 1
128	    ;;
129	esac
130	
131	echo "Deployment complete (target: ${DEPLOY_TARGET})"
132

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

################################################################################
# Deploy script with ECS/EKS abstraction layer
# Usage: DEPLOY_TARGET=ecs ./deploy.sh [service_name]
# Examples:
#   DEPLOY_TARGET=ecs ./deploy.sh clickhouse
#   DEPLOY_TARGET=ecs ./deploy.sh keeper
#   DEPLOY_TARGET=ecs ./deploy.sh all
################################################################################

DEPLOY_TARGET="${DEPLOY_TARGET:-ecs}"
SERVICE_NAME="${1:-all}"
PROJECT="${PROJECT:-logplatform}"
ENVIRONMENT="${ENVIRONMENT:-dev}"
AWS_REGION="${AWS_REGION:-ap-northeast-1}"

CLUSTER_NAME="${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}"

# ClickHouse and Keeper service names
CLICKHOUSE_SERVICES=(
  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-ch-s1r1"
  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-ch-s1r2"
  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-ch-s2r1"
  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-ch-s2r2"
)
KEEPER_SERVICES=(
  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-keeper-1"
  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-keeper-2"
  "${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}-keeper-3"
)

################################################################################
# Abstraction functions
################################################################################

deploy_ecs_service() {
  local service_name="$1"
  echo "Deploying ECS service: ${service_name}"
  aws ecs update-service \
    --cluster "${CLUSTER_NAME}" \
    --service "${service_name}" \
    --force-new-deployment \
    --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
    --no-cli-pager
}

deploy_eks_service() {
  local service_name="$1"
  echo "Deploying EKS service: ${service_name}"
  kubectl rollout restart "statefulset/${service_name}" -n "${PROJECT}"
}

wait_ecs_stable() {
  local service_name="$1"
  echo "Waiting for ECS service to stabilize: ${service_name}"
  aws ecs wait services-stable \
    --cluster "${CLUSTER_NAME}" \
    --services "${service_name}" \
    --region "${AWS_REGION}"
}

wait_eks_stable() {
  local service_name="$1"
  echo "Waiting for EKS rollout: ${service_name}"
  kubectl rollout status "statefulset/${service_name}" -n "${PROJECT}" --timeout=300s
}

deploy_service() {
  local service_name="$1"
  case "${DEPLOY_TARGET}" in
    ecs) deploy_ecs_service "${service_name}" ;;
    eks) deploy_eks_service "${service_name}" ;;
    *)   echo "ERROR: Unknown DEPLOY_TARGET: ${DEPLOY_TARGET}"; exit 1 ;;
  esac
}

wait_stable() {
  local service_name="$1"
  case "${DEPLOY_TARGET}" in
    ecs) wait_ecs_stable "${service_name}" ;;
    eks) wait_eks_stable "${service_name}" ;;
  esac
}

################################################################################
# Deploy logic
################################################################################

deploy_keeper() {
  echo "=== Deploying Keeper services ==="
  for svc in "${KEEPER_SERVICES[@]}"; do
    deploy_service "${svc}"
  done
  for svc in "${KEEPER_SERVICES[@]}"; do
    wait_stable "${svc}"
  done
  echo "=== Keeper deployment complete ==="
}

deploy_clickhouse() {
  echo "=== Deploying ClickHouse services ==="
  for svc in "${CLICKHOUSE_SERVICES[@]}"; do
    deploy_service "${svc}"
  done
  for svc in "${CLICKHOUSE_SERVICES[@]}"; do
    wait_stable "${svc}"
  done
  echo "=== ClickHouse deployment complete ==="
}

case "${SERVICE_NAME}" in
  keeper)
    deploy_keeper
    ;;
  clickhouse)
    deploy_clickhouse
    ;;
  all)
    deploy_keeper
    deploy_clickhouse
    ;;
  *)
    echo "ERROR: Unknown service: ${SERVICE_NAME}"
    echo "Usage: $0 [keeper|clickhouse|all]"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

echo "Deployment complete (target: ${DEPLOY_TARGET})"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

################################################################################
# Health check script for ClickHouse cluster
# Usage: ./health-check.sh [endpoint] [password]
# Default endpoint: localhost
################################################################################

ENDPOINT="${1:-localhost}"
CH_PASSWORD="${2:-${CH_DEFAULT_PASSWORD:-}}"
CH_HTTP_PORT="${CH_HTTP_PORT:-8123}"
EXIT_CODE=0

if [ -z "$CH_PASSWORD" ]; then
  echo "WARN: No password provided. Set CH_DEFAULT_PASSWORD or pass as second argument."
fi

AUTH_OPTS=""
if [ -n "$CH_PASSWORD" ]; then
  AUTH_OPTS="--user default:${CH_PASSWORD}"
fi

echo "=== ClickHouse Cluster Health Check ==="
echo "Endpoint: ${ENDPOINT}:${CH_HTTP_PORT}"
echo ""

# 1. Basic ping (no auth required)
echo "--- Ping ---"
if curl -sf "http://${ENDPOINT}:${CH_HTTP_PORT}/ping" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "OK: ClickHouse is responding"
else
  echo "FAIL: ClickHouse is not responding"
  EXIT_CODE=1
fi

# 2. Cluster status
echo ""
echo "--- Cluster Status ---"
curl -sf "http://${ENDPOINT}:${CH_HTTP_PORT}" \
  ${AUTH_OPTS} \
  --data "SELECT cluster, shard_num, replica_num, host_name, is_local FROM system.clusters WHERE cluster = 'logs_cluster' FORMAT PrettyCompact" \
  2>/dev/null || { echo "FAIL: Cannot query cluster status"; EXIT_CODE=1; }

# 3. Replication status
echo ""
echo "--- Replication Queue ---"
QUEUE_SIZE=$(curl -sf "http://${ENDPOINT}:${CH_HTTP_PORT}" \
  ${AUTH_OPTS} \
  --data "SELECT count() FROM system.replication_queue" 2>/dev/null || echo "ERROR")
if [ "${QUEUE_SIZE}" = "ERROR" ]; then
  echo "FAIL: Cannot query replication queue"
  EXIT_CODE=1
elif [ "${QUEUE_SIZE}" -gt 100 ]; then
  echo "WARN: Replication queue size is ${QUEUE_SIZE} (threshold: 100)"
else
  echo "OK: Replication queue size: ${QUEUE_SIZE}"
fi

# 4. Keeper connectivity
echo ""
echo "--- Keeper Status ---"
curl -sf "http://${ENDPOINT}:${CH_HTTP_PORT}" \
  ${AUTH_OPTS} \
  --data "SELECT * FROM system.zookeeper WHERE path = '/' LIMIT 1 FORMAT PrettyCompact" \
  2>/dev/null && echo "OK: Keeper is connected" || { echo "FAIL: Keeper is not connected"; EXIT_CODE=1; }

# 5. S3 storage status
echo ""
echo "--- Storage Disks ---"
curl -sf "http://${ENDPOINT}:${CH_HTTP_PORT}" \
  ${AUTH_OPTS} \
  --data "SELECT name, type, path, free_space, total_space FROM system.disks FORMAT PrettyCompact" \
  2>/dev/null || { echo "FAIL: Cannot query disk status"; EXIT_CODE=1; }

echo ""
echo "=== Health check complete (exit code: ${EXIT_CODE}) ==="
exit ${EXIT_CODE}

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

################################################################################
# Rolling update script for ClickHouse / Keeper on ECS
#
# Usage:
#   ./rolling-update.sh [keeper|clickhouse|all]
#
# Environment variables:
#   PROJECT        (default: logplatform)
#   ENVIRONMENT    (default: dev)
#   AWS_REGION     (default: ap-northeast-1)
#   CH_PASSWORD    (required for ClickHouse health checks)
#   DRY_RUN        (default: false) - set to "true" to preview without deploying
#
# Prerequisites:
#   - aws CLI configured with appropriate credentials
#   - nc (nmap-ncat) available on a host that can reach Keeper/ClickHouse FQDNs
#     (run this script from CloudShell VPC environment or a bastion host)
################################################################################

TARGET="${1:-all}"
PROJECT="${PROJECT:-logplatform}"
ENVIRONMENT="${ENVIRONMENT:-dev}"
AWS_REGION="${AWS_REGION:-ap-northeast-1}"
CH_PASSWORD="${CH_PASSWORD:-}"
DRY_RUN="${DRY_RUN:-false}"

CLUSTER_NAME="${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}"
DNS_SUFFIX="${PROJECT}.local"

# Service and FQDN mappings
KEEPER_IDS=(1 2 3)
CH_NODES=(s1r1 s1r2 s2r1 s2r2)

# Rolling order: update one replica per shard at a time to maintain availability
# Round 1: s1r1, s2r1 (one replica from each shard)
# Round 2: s1r2, s2r2 (the other replica from each shard)
CH_ROUND_1=(s1r1 s2r1)
CH_ROUND_2=(s1r2 s2r2)

################################################################################
# Utilities
################################################################################

log()  { echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] $*"; }
info() { log "INFO  $*"; }
warn() { log "WARN  $*"; }
err()  { log "ERROR $*"; }
sep()  { echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"; }

abort() {
  err "$1"
  err "Rolling update aborted. Already-updated nodes are running the new version."
  err "Fix the issue and re-run the script to continue."
  exit 1
}

dry_run_guard() {
  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
    info "[DRY RUN] Would execute: $*"
    return 0
  fi
  return 1
}

################################################################################
# ECS operations
################################################################################

deploy_ecs_service() {
  local service_name="${CLUSTER_NAME}-$1"
  if dry_run_guard "aws ecs update-service --force-new-deployment ${service_name}"; then
    return 0
  fi
  info "Deploying: ${service_name}"
  aws ecs update-service \
    --cluster "${CLUSTER_NAME}" \
    --service "${service_name}" \
    --force-new-deployment \
    --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
    --no-cli-pager > /dev/null
}

wait_ecs_stable() {
  local service_name="${CLUSTER_NAME}-$1"
  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then return 0; fi
  info "Waiting for service to stabilize: ${service_name}"
  aws ecs wait services-stable \
    --cluster "${CLUSTER_NAME}" \
    --services "${service_name}" \
    --region "${AWS_REGION}"
  info "Service stabilized: ${service_name}"
}

################################################################################
# Health checks
################################################################################

check_keeper_health() {
  local keeper_id="$1"
  local fqdn="keeper-${keeper_id}.${DNS_SUFFIX}"
  local max_attempts=60
  local attempt=0

  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
    info "[DRY RUN] Would check keeper-${keeper_id} health"
    return 0
  fi

  info "Checking keeper-${keeper_id} health (${fqdn})..."
  while [ $attempt -lt $max_attempts ]; do
    if echo "ruok" | nc -w 2 "${fqdn}" 9181 2>/dev/null | grep -q "imok"; then
      info "keeper-${keeper_id}: healthy (imok)"
      return 0
    fi
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    if [ $((attempt % 6)) -eq 0 ]; then
      info "  Still waiting for keeper-${keeper_id}... (${attempt}/${max_attempts})"
    fi
    sleep 5
  done

  abort "keeper-${keeper_id} did not become healthy within $((max_attempts * 5))s"
}

check_keeper_quorum() {
  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
    info "[DRY RUN] Would verify Keeper quorum"
    return 0
  fi

  info "Verifying Keeper quorum..."
  local healthy=0
  for id in "${KEEPER_IDS[@]}"; do
    local fqdn="keeper-${id}.${DNS_SUFFIX}"
    if echo "ruok" | nc -w 2 "${fqdn}" 9181 2>/dev/null | grep -q "imok"; then
      healthy=$((healthy + 1))
    else
      warn "keeper-${id} is not responding"
    fi
  done

  if [ $healthy -lt 2 ]; then
    abort "Keeper quorum lost (${healthy}/3 healthy). Need at least 2."
  fi
  info "Keeper quorum OK (${healthy}/3 healthy)"
}

get_keeper_znode_count() {
  local keeper_id="$1"
  local fqdn="keeper-${keeper_id}.${DNS_SUFFIX}"
  echo "mntr" | nc -w 2 "${fqdn}" 9181 2>/dev/null | grep "zk_znode_count" | awk '{print $2}'
}

check_clickhouse_health() {
  local node="$1"
  local fqdn
  local max_attempts=60
  local attempt=0

  case "${node}" in
    s1r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s1r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
  esac

  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
    info "[DRY RUN] Would check ClickHouse ${node} health (${fqdn})"
    return 0
  fi

  info "Checking ClickHouse ${node} health (${fqdn})..."
  while [ $attempt -lt $max_attempts ]; do
    if curl -sf "http://${fqdn}:8123/ping" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
      info "ClickHouse ${node}: ping OK"
      return 0
    fi
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    if [ $((attempt % 6)) -eq 0 ]; then
      info "  Still waiting for ${node}... (${attempt}/${max_attempts})"
    fi
    sleep 5
  done

  abort "ClickHouse ${node} did not become healthy within $((max_attempts * 5))s"
}

check_replication_queue() {
  local node="$1"
  local fqdn
  local max_attempts=60
  local attempt=0

  case "${node}" in
    s1r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s1r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
  esac

  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
    info "[DRY RUN] Would check replication queue on ${node}"
    return 0
  fi

  if [ -z "${CH_PASSWORD}" ]; then
    warn "CH_PASSWORD not set, skipping replication queue check"
    return 0
  fi

  info "Waiting for replication queue to drain on ${node}..."
  while [ $attempt -lt $max_attempts ]; do
    local queue_size
    queue_size=$(curl -sf "http://${fqdn}:8123" \
      --user "default:${CH_PASSWORD}" \
      --data "SELECT count() FROM system.replication_queue" 2>/dev/null || echo "ERROR")

    if [ "${queue_size}" = "ERROR" ]; then
      attempt=$((attempt + 1))
      sleep 5
      continue
    fi

    if [ "${queue_size}" -le 10 ]; then
      info "Replication queue on ${node}: ${queue_size} (OK)"
      return 0
    fi

    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    if [ $((attempt % 6)) -eq 0 ]; then
      info "  Queue size: ${queue_size}, waiting... (${attempt}/${max_attempts})"
    fi
    sleep 5
  done

  warn "Replication queue on ${node} did not drain within $((max_attempts * 5))s (proceeding anyway)"
}

check_no_readonly_replicas() {
  local node="$1"
  local fqdn

  case "${node}" in
    s1r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s1r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
  esac

  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ] || [ -z "${CH_PASSWORD}" ]; then
    return 0
  fi

  local readonly_count
  readonly_count=$(curl -sf "http://${fqdn}:8123" \
    --user "default:${CH_PASSWORD}" \
    --data "SELECT count() FROM system.replicas WHERE is_readonly = 1" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

  if [ "${readonly_count}" != "0" ]; then
    abort "ClickHouse ${node} has ${readonly_count} readonly replica(s)"
  fi
  info "ClickHouse ${node}: no readonly replicas"
}

################################################################################
# Rolling update: Keeper
################################################################################

rolling_update_keeper() {
  sep
  info "Starting Keeper rolling update"
  sep

  # Pre-flight: verify quorum
  check_keeper_quorum

  # Record initial znode counts
  local initial_znodes=()
  for id in "${KEEPER_IDS[@]}"; do
    local count
    count=$(get_keeper_znode_count "$id")
    initial_znodes+=("${count}")
    info "keeper-${id} znode_count: ${count}"
  done

  # Roll one node at a time
  for i in "${!KEEPER_IDS[@]}"; do
    local id="${KEEPER_IDS[$i]}"
    sep
    info ">>> Updating keeper-${id} (${i+1}/${#KEEPER_IDS[@]})"

    deploy_ecs_service "keeper-${id}"
    wait_ecs_stable "keeper-${id}"
    check_keeper_health "${id}"
    check_keeper_quorum

    # Verify znode count recovered
    if [ "${DRY_RUN}" != "true" ]; then
      local new_count
      new_count=$(get_keeper_znode_count "$id")
      info "keeper-${id} znode_count: ${new_count} (was: ${initial_znodes[$i]})"
      if [ -n "${new_count}" ] && [ -n "${initial_znodes[$i]}" ] && [ "${new_count}" -lt "${initial_znodes[$i]}" ]; then
        warn "znode_count decreased on keeper-${id} (${initial_znodes[$i]} -> ${new_count})"
      fi
    fi

    info "<<< keeper-${id} update complete"
  done

  sep
  info "Keeper rolling update complete"
  sep
}

################################################################################
# Rolling update: ClickHouse
################################################################################

rolling_update_clickhouse() {
  sep
  info "Starting ClickHouse rolling update"
  sep

  if [ -z "${CH_PASSWORD}" ]; then
    warn "CH_PASSWORD not set. Replication queue and readonly checks will be skipped."
  fi

  # Pre-flight: check all nodes healthy
  for node in "${CH_NODES[@]}"; do
    check_clickhouse_health "${node}"
  done

  # Round 1: one replica from each shard
  sep
  info ">>> Round 1: ${CH_ROUND_1[*]}"
  for node in "${CH_ROUND_1[@]}"; do
    info "Updating ${node}..."
    deploy_ecs_service "ch-${node}"
  done
  for node in "${CH_ROUND_1[@]}"; do
    wait_ecs_stable "ch-${node}"
    check_clickhouse_health "${node}"
    check_replication_queue "${node}"
    check_no_readonly_replicas "${node}"
  done
  info "<<< Round 1 complete"

  # Round 2: the other replica from each shard
  sep
  info ">>> Round 2: ${CH_ROUND_2[*]}"
  for node in "${CH_ROUND_2[@]}"; do
    info "Updating ${node}..."
    deploy_ecs_service "ch-${node}"
  done
  for node in "${CH_ROUND_2[@]}"; do
    wait_ecs_stable "ch-${node}"
    check_clickhouse_health "${node}"
    check_replication_queue "${node}"
    check_no_readonly_replicas "${node}"
  done
  info "<<< Round 2 complete"

  # Final verification
  sep
  info "Final verification: all nodes"
  for node in "${CH_NODES[@]}"; do
    check_no_readonly_replicas "${node}"
  done

  sep
  info "ClickHouse rolling update complete"
  sep
}

################################################################################
# Version reporting
################################################################################

report_versions() {
  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ] || [ -z "${CH_PASSWORD}" ]; then
    return 0
  fi

  sep
  info "Cluster version report"
  sep
  for node in "${CH_NODES[@]}"; do
    local fqdn
    case "${node}" in
      s1r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
      s1r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
      s2r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
      s2r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    esac
    local version
    version=$(curl -sf "http://${fqdn}:8123" \
      --user "default:${CH_PASSWORD}" \
      --data "SELECT version()" 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A")
    info "${node} (${fqdn}): ${version}"
  done

  for id in "${KEEPER_IDS[@]}"; do
    local fqdn="keeper-${id}.${DNS_SUFFIX}"
    local version
    version=$(echo "stat" | nc -w 2 "${fqdn}" 9181 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "N/A")
    info "keeper-${id} (${fqdn}): ${version}"
  done
}

################################################################################
# Main
################################################################################

info "Rolling update target: ${TARGET}"
info "Cluster: ${CLUSTER_NAME} (region: ${AWS_REGION})"
if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
  info "*** DRY RUN MODE - no changes will be made ***"
fi
echo ""

case "${TARGET}" in
  keeper)
    rolling_update_keeper
    ;;
  clickhouse)
    rolling_update_clickhouse
    report_versions
    ;;
  all)
    rolling_update_keeper
    rolling_update_clickhouse
    report_versions
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 [keeper|clickhouse|all]"
    echo ""
    echo "Environment variables:"
    echo "  PROJECT        Project name (default: logplatform)"
    echo "  ENVIRONMENT    Environment (default: dev)"
    echo "  AWS_REGION     AWS region (default: ap-northeast-1)"
    echo "  CH_PASSWORD    ClickHouse default user password (required for full checks)"
    echo "  DRY_RUN        Set to 'true' to preview without deploying"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

info "Done."

toolUseResult.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

################################################################################
# Rolling update script for ClickHouse / Keeper on ECS
#
# Usage:
#   ./rolling-update.sh [keeper|clickhouse|all]
#
# Environment variables:
#   PROJECT        (default: logplatform)
#   ENVIRONMENT    (default: dev)
#   AWS_REGION     (default: ap-northeast-1)
#   CH_PASSWORD    (required for ClickHouse health checks)
#   DRY_RUN        (default: false) - set to "true" to preview without deploying
#
# Prerequisites:
#   - aws CLI configured with appropriate credentials
#   - nc (nmap-ncat) available on a host that can reach Keeper/ClickHouse FQDNs
#     (run this script from CloudShell VPC environment or a bastion host)
################################################################################

TARGET="${1:-all}"
PROJECT="${PROJECT:-logplatform}"
ENVIRONMENT="${ENVIRONMENT:-dev}"
AWS_REGION="${AWS_REGION:-ap-northeast-1}"
CH_PASSWORD="${CH_PASSWORD:-}"
DRY_RUN="${DRY_RUN:-false}"

CLUSTER_NAME="${PROJECT}-${ENVIRONMENT}"
DNS_SUFFIX="${PROJECT}.local"

# Service and FQDN mappings
KEEPER_IDS=(1 2 3)
CH_NODES=(s1r1 s1r2 s2r1 s2r2)

# Rolling order: update one replica per shard at a time to maintain availability
# Round 1: s1r1, s2r1 (one replica from each shard)
# Round 2: s1r2, s2r2 (the other replica from each shard)
CH_ROUND_1=(s1r1 s2r1)
CH_ROUND_2=(s1r2 s2r2)

################################################################################
# Utilities
################################################################################

log()  { echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] $*"; }
info() { log "INFO  $*"; }
warn() { log "WARN  $*"; }
err()  { log "ERROR $*"; }
sep()  { echo "────────────────────────────────────────────────────────"; }

abort() {
  err "$1"
  err "Rolling update aborted. Already-updated nodes are running the new version."
  err "Fix the issue and re-run the script to continue."
  exit 1
}

dry_run_guard() {
  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
    info "[DRY RUN] Would execute: $*"
    return 0
  fi
  return 1
}

################################################################################
# ECS operations
################################################################################

deploy_ecs_service() {
  local service_name="${CLUSTER_NAME}-$1"
  if dry_run_guard "aws ecs update-service --force-new-deployment ${service_name}"; then
    return 0
  fi
  info "Deploying: ${service_name}"
  aws ecs update-service \
    --cluster "${CLUSTER_NAME}" \
    --service "${service_name}" \
    --force-new-deployment \
    --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
    --no-cli-pager > /dev/null
}

wait_ecs_stable() {
  local service_name="${CLUSTER_NAME}-$1"
  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then return 0; fi
  info "Waiting for service to stabilize: ${service_name}"
  aws ecs wait services-stable \
    --cluster "${CLUSTER_NAME}" \
    --services "${service_name}" \
    --region "${AWS_REGION}"
  info "Service stabilized: ${service_name}"
}

################################################################################
# Health checks
################################################################################

check_keeper_health() {
  local keeper_id="$1"
  local fqdn="keeper-${keeper_id}.${DNS_SUFFIX}"
  local max_attempts=60
  local attempt=0

  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
    info "[DRY RUN] Would check keeper-${keeper_id} health"
    return 0
  fi

  info "Checking keeper-${keeper_id} health (${fqdn})..."
  while [ $attempt -lt $max_attempts ]; do
    if echo "ruok" | nc -w 2 "${fqdn}" 9181 2>/dev/null | grep -q "imok"; then
      info "keeper-${keeper_id}: healthy (imok)"
      return 0
    fi
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    if [ $((attempt % 6)) -eq 0 ]; then
      info "  Still waiting for keeper-${keeper_id}... (${attempt}/${max_attempts})"
    fi
    sleep 5
  done

  abort "keeper-${keeper_id} did not become healthy within $((max_attempts * 5))s"
}

check_keeper_quorum() {
  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
    info "[DRY RUN] Would verify Keeper quorum"
    return 0
  fi

  info "Verifying Keeper quorum..."
  local healthy=0
  for id in "${KEEPER_IDS[@]}"; do
    local fqdn="keeper-${id}.${DNS_SUFFIX}"
    if echo "ruok" | nc -w 2 "${fqdn}" 9181 2>/dev/null | grep -q "imok"; then
      healthy=$((healthy + 1))
    else
      warn "keeper-${id} is not responding"
    fi
  done

  if [ $healthy -lt 2 ]; then
    abort "Keeper quorum lost (${healthy}/3 healthy). Need at least 2."
  fi
  info "Keeper quorum OK (${healthy}/3 healthy)"
}

get_keeper_znode_count() {
  local keeper_id="$1"
  local fqdn="keeper-${keeper_id}.${DNS_SUFFIX}"
  echo "mntr" | nc -w 2 "${fqdn}" 9181 2>/dev/null | grep "zk_znode_count" | awk '{print $2}'
}

check_clickhouse_health() {
  local node="$1"
  local fqdn
  local max_attempts=60
  local attempt=0

  case "${node}" in
    s1r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s1r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
  esac

  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
    info "[DRY RUN] Would check ClickHouse ${node} health (${fqdn})"
    return 0
  fi

  info "Checking ClickHouse ${node} health (${fqdn})..."
  while [ $attempt -lt $max_attempts ]; do
    if curl -sf "http://${fqdn}:8123/ping" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
      info "ClickHouse ${node}: ping OK"
      return 0
    fi
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    if [ $((attempt % 6)) -eq 0 ]; then
      info "  Still waiting for ${node}... (${attempt}/${max_attempts})"
    fi
    sleep 5
  done

  abort "ClickHouse ${node} did not become healthy within $((max_attempts * 5))s"
}

check_replication_queue() {
  local node="$1"
  local fqdn
  local max_attempts=60
  local attempt=0

  case "${node}" in
    s1r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s1r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
  esac

  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
    info "[DRY RUN] Would check replication queue on ${node}"
    return 0
  fi

  if [ -z "${CH_PASSWORD}" ]; then
    warn "CH_PASSWORD not set, skipping replication queue check"
    return 0
  fi

  info "Waiting for replication queue to drain on ${node}..."
  while [ $attempt -lt $max_attempts ]; do
    local queue_size
    queue_size=$(curl -sf "http://${fqdn}:8123" \
      --user "default:${CH_PASSWORD}" \
      --data "SELECT count() FROM system.replication_queue" 2>/dev/null || echo "ERROR")

    if [ "${queue_size}" = "ERROR" ]; then
      attempt=$((attempt + 1))
      sleep 5
      continue
    fi

    if [ "${queue_size}" -le 10 ]; then
      info "Replication queue on ${node}: ${queue_size} (OK)"
      return 0
    fi

    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    if [ $((attempt % 6)) -eq 0 ]; then
      info "  Queue size: ${queue_size}, waiting... (${attempt}/${max_attempts})"
    fi
    sleep 5
  done

  warn "Replication queue on ${node} did not drain within $((max_attempts * 5))s (proceeding anyway)"
}

check_no_readonly_replicas() {
  local node="$1"
  local fqdn

  case "${node}" in
    s1r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s1r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    s2r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
  esac

  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ] || [ -z "${CH_PASSWORD}" ]; then
    return 0
  fi

  local readonly_count
  readonly_count=$(curl -sf "http://${fqdn}:8123" \
    --user "default:${CH_PASSWORD}" \
    --data "SELECT count() FROM system.replicas WHERE is_readonly = 1" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

  if [ "${readonly_count}" != "0" ]; then
    abort "ClickHouse ${node} has ${readonly_count} readonly replica(s)"
  fi
  info "ClickHouse ${node}: no readonly replicas"
}

################################################################################
# Rolling update: Keeper
################################################################################

rolling_update_keeper() {
  sep
  info "Starting Keeper rolling update"
  sep

  # Pre-flight: verify quorum
  check_keeper_quorum

  # Record initial znode counts
  local initial_znodes=()
  for id in "${KEEPER_IDS[@]}"; do
    local count
    count=$(get_keeper_znode_count "$id")
    initial_znodes+=("${count}")
    info "keeper-${id} znode_count: ${count}"
  done

  # Roll one node at a time
  for i in "${!KEEPER_IDS[@]}"; do
    local id="${KEEPER_IDS[$i]}"
    sep
    info ">>> Updating keeper-${id} (${i+1}/${#KEEPER_IDS[@]})"

    deploy_ecs_service "keeper-${id}"
    wait_ecs_stable "keeper-${id}"
    check_keeper_health "${id}"
    check_keeper_quorum

    # Verify znode count recovered
    if [ "${DRY_RUN}" != "true" ]; then
      local new_count
      new_count=$(get_keeper_znode_count "$id")
      info "keeper-${id} znode_count: ${new_count} (was: ${initial_znodes[$i]})"
      if [ -n "${new_count}" ] && [ -n "${initial_znodes[$i]}" ] && [ "${new_count}" -lt "${initial_znodes[$i]}" ]; then
        warn "znode_count decreased on keeper-${id} (${initial_znodes[$i]} -> ${new_count})"
      fi
    fi

    info "<<< keeper-${id} update complete"
  done

  sep
  info "Keeper rolling update complete"
  sep
}

################################################################################
# Rolling update: ClickHouse
################################################################################

rolling_update_clickhouse() {
  sep
  info "Starting ClickHouse rolling update"
  sep

  if [ -z "${CH_PASSWORD}" ]; then
    warn "CH_PASSWORD not set. Replication queue and readonly checks will be skipped."
  fi

  # Pre-flight: check all nodes healthy
  for node in "${CH_NODES[@]}"; do
    check_clickhouse_health "${node}"
  done

  # Round 1: one replica from each shard
  sep
  info ">>> Round 1: ${CH_ROUND_1[*]}"
  for node in "${CH_ROUND_1[@]}"; do
    info "Updating ${node}..."
    deploy_ecs_service "ch-${node}"
  done
  for node in "${CH_ROUND_1[@]}"; do
    wait_ecs_stable "ch-${node}"
    check_clickhouse_health "${node}"
    check_replication_queue "${node}"
    check_no_readonly_replicas "${node}"
  done
  info "<<< Round 1 complete"

  # Round 2: the other replica from each shard
  sep
  info ">>> Round 2: ${CH_ROUND_2[*]}"
  for node in "${CH_ROUND_2[@]}"; do
    info "Updating ${node}..."
    deploy_ecs_service "ch-${node}"
  done
  for node in "${CH_ROUND_2[@]}"; do
    wait_ecs_stable "ch-${node}"
    check_clickhouse_health "${node}"
    check_replication_queue "${node}"
    check_no_readonly_replicas "${node}"
  done
  info "<<< Round 2 complete"

  # Final verification
  sep
  info "Final verification: all nodes"
  for node in "${CH_NODES[@]}"; do
    check_no_readonly_replicas "${node}"
  done

  sep
  info "ClickHouse rolling update complete"
  sep
}

################################################################################
# Version reporting
################################################################################

report_versions() {
  if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ] || [ -z "${CH_PASSWORD}" ]; then
    return 0
  fi

  sep
  info "Cluster version report"
  sep
  for node in "${CH_NODES[@]}"; do
    local fqdn
    case "${node}" in
      s1r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
      s1r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard1-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
      s2r1) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica1.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
      s2r2) fqdn="clickhouse-shard2-replica2.${DNS_SUFFIX}" ;;
    esac
    local version
    version=$(curl -sf "http://${fqdn}:8123" \
      --user "default:${CH_PASSWORD}" \
      --data "SELECT version()" 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A")
    info "${node} (${fqdn}): ${version}"
  done

  for id in "${KEEPER_IDS[@]}"; do
    local fqdn="keeper-${id}.${DNS_SUFFIX}"
    local version
    version=$(echo "stat" | nc -w 2 "${fqdn}" 9181 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "N/A")
    info "keeper-${id} (${fqdn}): ${version}"
  done
}

################################################################################
# Main
################################################################################

info "Rolling update target: ${TARGET}"
info "Cluster: ${CLUSTER_NAME} (region: ${AWS_REGION})"
if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = "true" ]; then
  info "*** DRY RUN MODE - no changes will be made ***"
fi
echo ""

case "${TARGET}" in
  keeper)
    rolling_update_keeper
    ;;
  clickhouse)
    rolling_update_clickhouse
    report_versions
    ;;
  all)
    rolling_update_keeper
    rolling_update_clickhouse
    report_versions
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: $0 [keeper|clickhouse|all]"
    echo ""
    echo "Environment variables:"
    echo "  PROJECT        Project name (default: logplatform)"
    echo "  ENVIRONMENT    Environment (default: dev)"
    echo "  AWS_REGION     AWS region (default: ap-northeast-1)"
    echo "  CH_PASSWORD    ClickHouse default user password (required for full checks)"
    echo "  DRY_RUN        Set to 'true' to preview without deploying"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

info "Done."

content.text:
ShellCheck通過。スクリプトの設計を説明します。

### `rolling-update.sh` の設計

**Keeper（1ノードずつ順次）**:
```
keeper-1 → deploy → stable待ち → ruok確認 → quorum確認 → znode数検証
keeper-2 → deploy → stable待ち → ruok確認 → quorum確認 → znode数検証
keeper-3 → deploy → stable待ち → ruok確認 → quorum確認 → znode数検証
```

**ClickHouse（2ラウンド制、各シャードから1レプリカずつ）**:
```
Round 1: s1r1 + s2r1 を同時デプロイ → 各ノードのstable待ち → ping → レプリケーションキュー消化 → readonly確認
Round 2: s1r2 + s2r2 を同時デプロイ → 同上
最終検証: 全4ノードのreadonly確認 + バージョンレポート
```

各シャードで常に1レプリカが稼働するため、サービス無停止です。

**使い方**:
```bash
# ドライラン（実際にはデプロイしない）
DRY_RUN=true CH_PASSWORD=xxx ./rolling-update.sh all

# Keeperのみ
CH_PASSWORD=xxx ./rolling-update.sh keeper

# ClickHouseのみ
CH_PASSWORD=xxx ./rolling-update.sh clickhouse

# 全体
CH_PASSWORD=xxx ./rolling-update.sh all
```

**安全策**:
- 各ステップでヘルスチェックに失敗するとabortし、途中状態を明示
- Keeperはquorum（2/3）を毎ステップ検証
- ClickHouseはreadonlyレプリカの検出で中断
- `DRY_RUN=true`でプレビュー可能

Extract all settings related to rolling updates, update strategy, pod management policy, and any configuration that controls how ClickHouse nodes are updated. Include any health check, readiness probe, or liveness probe configurations.

content.content:
# ClickHouse Operator Key Features

Based on the repository documentation, here are the relevant features:

## Core Update & Management Capabilities

**Version Management:**
- "ClickHouse version upgrades" as a core feature
- Documentation on "Update ClickHouse version" procedures
- Operator version update guidance available

**Cluster Operations:**
- "Creates ClickHouse clusters defined as custom resources"
- "ClickHouse cluster scaling including automatic schema propagation"
- Support for adding replication to existing clusters

**Configuration Management:**
- Customizable storage provisioning via VolumeClaim templates
- Customizable pod templates for infrastructure control
- "ClickHouse configuration management" capabilities
- "ClickHouse users management" functionality

## Safety & Reliability Features

**Monitoring Integration:**
- Prometheus metrics export functionality
- Grafana dashboard integration available
- Observable cluster health through metrics

**Replication Support:**
- Dedicated replication setup documentation
- Zookeeper integration for distributed coordination
- Schema propagation during scaling operations

**Documentation-Based Safety:**
The operator provides comprehensive guides for maintenance tasks, suggesting thoughtful update procedures rather than automatic unsafe operations.

## Notable Limitations

The README lists features but doesn't explicitly detail rolling update strategies, health check mechanisms, or stateful set update configurations—these likely exist in detailed documentation not shown in the provided content.

content.content:
# ClickHouse Rolling Update Configuration

## Update Strategy Settings

The configuration specifies reconciliation behaviors for StatefulSet updates:

**Update Timeout & Polling:**
- "How many seconds to wait for created/updated StatefulSet to be 'Ready': timeout: 300"
- Poll interval set to 5-second checks between status verifications

**Failure Handling:**
The document outlines three scenarios with distinct failure policies:
- **Create failures:** "onFailure: ignore" - continues reconciliation despite creation issues
- **Update failures:** "onFailure: abort" - halts the process when updates don't reach ready state
- **Recreation scenarios:** "onDataLoss: recreate" and "onUpdateFailure: recreate"

## Host Reconciliation Controls

The configuration manages how hosts transition during updates:

**Wait Conditions:**
- Exclude hosts from cluster before updates (exclude: true)
- Wait for queries to complete (queries: true)  
- Skip waiting for host inclusion post-update (include: false)

**Replication Synchronization:**
- New replicas explicitly wait for replication catch-up
- Maximum acceptable replication lag: 10 seconds

## Pod Management Directives

**Rolling Update Trigger:**
- "restart: RollingUpdate" - forces pod restarts during every reconciliation cycle

**Pod Distribution Policies:**
The template supports anti-affinity rules (ShardAntiAffinity, ReplicaAntiAffinity) ensuring "one replica of a shard instance per topologyKey-specified unit"

## Health Monitoring

**Probe Configuration:**
- Startup probe: disabled (startup: no)
- Readiness probe: enabled (readiness: yes)
- Probes are skipped if unspecified in pod definitions
